hero_summary: Shadd Araban is a branch of the Hijaz family, and its personality emerges through a distinctive upper route and a stronger contrast inside the family, while the Hijaz color remains present in the foundation and the sayr.
short_identity: A Hijaz branch with a clear upper path and a stronger auditory contrast than some other branches of the family.

quick_facts.karar: The tonic according to the chosen root
quick_facts.ghammaz: Its importance is determined by the route and upper treatment
quick_facts.lower_jins: Jins Hijaz in the basic structure
quick_facts.upper_jins: An upper region with a more clearly contrasted path
quick_facts.character: Dramatic, distinct, and more strongly contrasted in the jawab
quick_facts.common_start: It usually begins by stabilizing the Hijaz foundation, then shows its identity through the upper path

what_is_it: Shadd Araban is read inside the Hijaz family as a branch that preserves the Hijaz essence, but gains its character through an upper construction that is more clearly contrasted than basic Hijaz.

lower.name: Jins Hijaz
lower.description: The lower Hijaz jins remains the basis that ties this branch to the Hijaz family.

upper.name: Contrasted upper region
upper.starts_on: Usually after the lower jins has been stabilized
upper.description: The identity of Shadd Araban appears through an upper path that is more distinctly contrasted inside the family.

jins_architecture.transition_note: The real difference between Shadd Araban and Hijaz appears in how the upper region is built and how tension is distributed there.

sayr.summary: Shadd Araban first stabilizes the Hijaz foundation, then moves into a more distinct upper construction before returning to the tonic to confirm its family belonging.
resting_tones: Resting tones within the lower jins
Upper stations that show stronger contrast in this branch
motion_notes: The difference from Hijaz is defined through the upper construction
One should listen closely to how the jawab is opened and how it returns

transpose_explainer: When this branch is transposed to another root, the interval logic and the functions of the tones that give it its identity inside the Hijaz family must be preserved. The written form, accidentals, and notational range may change, but the difference between this branch and Hijaz must remain audible in the upper region and the sayr.
historical_notes: Hijaz branches are commonly understood in modern teaching through their relation to the parent maqam and their difference from it in the upper region.
This branch is usually read inside the family more than as a separate system detached from Hijaz logic.
school_differences: The explanation of this branch may differ from one school or reference to another.
Some traditions emphasize the upper contrast, while others focus on the general structural reading.
common_confusions: Listing note names alone is not enough to understand this branch; one must pay attention to movement, repose, and upper coloring.
Similarity in the lower jins to Hijaz does not mean complete maqamic identity.
listening_notes: Start by comparing it with basic Hijaz.
Notice where the stronger auditory contrast appears in the upper region.
